description: When a referent's particular kind is unknown, irrelevant, or supplied elsewhere, 'thena' does the ordinary work of 'thing'. It can point to a physical object, an event, or a matter under discussion without claiming that all such referents share one substance. 'muralo' (material) asks what matter is in use; when the kind itself matters, a more specific noun does the job better.
articulatory_notes: Rest the tongue tip lightly against the upper teeth for unvoiced 'th', let air pass along it, and move into stressed true-mid front 'e'. Touch the tongue tip to the backs of the upper teeth for dental 'n', carry the voice through the nose, and finish with open central 'a'.
usage_notes: When 'thena' is counted, choose any optional classifier from the referent's actual nature rather than from the general word. A detached or crafted object may take 'themo'; an unclear or nonphysical referent may omit a classifier. A person can be a grammatical referent, but 'miona' (person) keeps personhood explicit whenever it is relevant and avoids reducing someone to an unspecified thing.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: General reference is useful when a category is unknown, but a person need not disappear inside it. 'miona' remains available whenever personhood, agency, or testimony matters.

semantic_domains.cognition: a general referent used when a more specific category is unknown or irrelevant
semantic_domains.physical: an unspecified object or material item when its particular kind is not needed
